nodes.title: The spellchecker doesnt have an option to add new words to dictionary
cozy-tree Encoding and File Reading
nodes.full_text:
Opening .gitignore with utf-8 is smart, but some edge-case files might have different encodings.
Suggestion: Use errors='ignore' in open() to handle rare malformed files gracefully.

nodes.title: The Nodes
nodes.full_text: They should auto resize on creating new nodes accordingly with the title width

nodes.title: cozy-tree .gitignore patterns
nodes.full_text:
Current implementation uses fnmatch, which handles basic patterns (*, ?, []) but falls short on advanced Git syntax like ** (recursive), ! (negate), or trailing / for directories only.
Suggestion: Consider integrating a library like pathspec (from GitPython or standalone) for accurate matching. It's lightweight and handles full .gitignore parsing. For example:Pythonfrom pathspec import PathSpec

# In _load_gitignore_patterns:
patterns = PathSpec.from_lines('gitwildmatch', f.readlines())
# Then in _should_ignore:
if patterns.match_file(rel_path):
    return TrueThis would make it "rock-solid" as promised, without much added complexity. Since your environment has no pip, you could implement a basic ** handler manually if needed, but pathspec might not be available—stick to fnmatch for now if simplicity is key.
